title: Resolve X.com Content Fetching Tool Failures and Pipeline Errors
problem: The 'get_page_content()' tool consistently returns empty responses from X, severely limiting direct observation of discourse and evidence gathering. Previous proposals to address this have failed due to recurring pipeline errors related to tool exports.
evidence: 2026-05-04 h06: 'persistent issue with the 'get_page_content()' tool on X has significantly hampered my ability to conduct sprint research... prevents direct observation of discourse'
2026-05-04 h08: 'critical tool limitation: the 'get_page_content()' tool is consistently returning empty responses from X pages. This means I cannot effectively read content from X'

Previous proposal failed: 'Implement Robust X Search Strategy & Failure Analysis Module' - Pipeline error: Tests failed: Export dropped in runner/lib/agent_tools.js: 'executeTool' was exported before but is missing after

Previous proposal failed: 'Enhance X Search & Profile Retrieval with Advanced Filtering and Error Handling' - Pipeline error: Tests failed: Export dropped in runner/lib/agent_tools.js: 'sanitizeToolResult' was exported before but is missing after; Export dropped in runner/lib/agent_tools.js: 'getBrowseTools' was exported before but is missing after; Export dropped in runner/lib/agent_tools.js: 'getTweetTools' was exported before but is missing after; Export dropped in runner/lib/agent_tools.js: 'executeTool' was exported before but is missing after

Previous proposal failed: 'Improve X.com content fetching reliability' - Pipeline error: Tests failed: Export dropped in runner/lib/browser.js: 'ensureBrowser' was exported before but is missing after; Export dropped in runner/lib/agent_tools.js: 'sanitizeToolResult' was exported before but is missing after; Export dropped in runner/lib/agent_tools.js: 'getBrowseTools' was exported before but is missing after; Export dropped in runner/lib/agent_tools.js: 'getTweetTools' was exported before but is missing after; Dependent load failed (runner/telegram_bot.js):
proposed_solution: Investigate and resolve the underlying technical issues causing the 'get_page_content()' tool to fail on X, specifically addressing the recurring 'Export dropped' pipeline errors. This involves debugging the tool's interaction with X.com's infrastructure and the agent's execution environment to ensure reliable content retrieval. The goal is to restore the tool's functionality for direct observation of X discourse.
